title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The heating element or its control circuit has failed, preventing water from reaching proper temperature.
fullDescription: Error code E01 on Thermador dishwashers indicates the control module has detected that water temperature is not rising as expected. As a BSH group product, Thermador shares diagnostic systems with Bosch and Gaggenau. The heater relay, heating element, or NTC sensor may be at fault. The dishwasher will attempt to continue with cold water but cleaning performance will be severely degraded.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or developed an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is providing incorrect readings
Control board heater relay has failed
Wiring harness between heater and control board is damaged
Thermal fuse on the heating element has tripped

symptoms: Dishes come out cold and greasy after a cycle
Water does not feel warm during the wash phase
E01 displayed on the control panel
Cycle takes significantly longer than normal
Sanitize light does not illuminate
Food residue remains on dishes after washing

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and disconnect power at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a test cycle to check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element at the bottom of the tub. Using a multimeter, measure resistance across the terminals. A functional element should read between 10-30 ohms. An open circuit indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor near the sump area. Disconnect and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 50k ohms and decrease with heat.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connections
fixSteps.instruction: Examine all wiring between the heating element, NTC sensor, and control board. Look for burned, corroded, or loose connections and repair as needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the element tested faulty, order a Thermador-compatible replacement. Remove the lower spray arm, disconnect wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new element.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected or if you are not comfortable working with electrical components near water. Thermador repairs may require authorized service for warranty coverage.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E01 error heating element fix

faq.q: Can I still use my Thermador dishwasher with an E01 error?
faq.a: The dishwasher may run but will wash with cold water, resulting in poor cleaning and no sanitization. Repair promptly.

faq.q: Is the Thermador E01 code the same as the Bosch E01?
faq.a: Yes. Thermador, Bosch, and Gaggenau are all BSH group brands and share the same diagnostic error code system.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a Thermador dishwasher E01?
faq.a: DIY repair costs $50-$120 for parts. Professional repair typically runs $200-$450 due to Thermador's premium service rates.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor is providing out-of-range readings to the control module.
fullDescription: Error code E02 on Thermador dishwashers indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) temperature sensor is sending readings outside the expected range. The control board cannot properly regulate water temperature, which impacts wash performance and sanitization. This BSH group code is shared across Thermador, Bosch, and Gaggenau dishwashers.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has failed or drifted out of calibration
Wiring to the NTC sensor is broken or corroded
Connector at the control board is loose or oxidized
Control board input circuit is damaged

symptoms: E02 error code on the display panel
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle unexpectedly
Water temperature seems inconsistent between cycles
Dishes not cleaned effectively
Cycle does not complete properly

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dishwasher from power for 5 minutes. Reconnect and start a cycle. If E02 returns, proceed to further diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate and access the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is typically in the sump area at the bottom of the tub. Remove the lower rack and spray arm, then access the sump cover.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor harness and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read around 50k ohms. Submerge in warm water and verify resistance decreases smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests faulty, disconnect the old sensor from the sump housing, install the new one, and reconnect the wiring harness. Run a test cycle to confirm.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not resolve the issue, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E02 NTC sensor replacement

faq.q: What does the NTC sensor do in a Thermador dishwasher?
faq.a: The NTC sensor monitors water temperature so the control board can regulate heating for optimal wash and sanitization cycles.

faq.q: Is E02 related to E01?
faq.a: They are related. E01 typically points to the heater itself while E02 specifically targets the temperature sensor, but both affect water heating.

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC sensor damage the dishwasher?
faq.a: It will not cause physical damage but cleaning performance will suffer significantly without proper temperature regulation.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher failed to fill with enough water within the allowed time period.
fullDescription: Error E03 is triggered when the Thermador dishwasher control board detects that the tub has not filled with adequate water within the allotted time (typically 5-8 minutes). The water level sensor indicates insufficient water despite the fill valve being activated. This can stem from supply issues, a faulty inlet valve, or a malfunctioning pressure switch.

causes.cause: Water supply valve to the dishwasher is closed or restricted
Water inlet valve is clogged or defective
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Water level sensor or pressure switch is faulty
Float switch is stuck in the raised position

symptoms: E03 error displayed on the panel
Dishwasher does not start the wash cycle
No sound of water entering the tub
Cycle starts but stops within minutes
Partial fill with very little water in the tub

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply valve under the sink is fully open. Run the kitchen faucet to confirm adequate water pressure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out enough to access the rear. Check the inlet hose for kinks, bends, or blockages. Straighten or replace if needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the water supply hose from the inlet valve. Remove and clean the small mesh filter screen. Debris buildup can restrict flow significantly.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the inlet valve solenoid coil for continuity. It should read between 500-1500 ohms. No continuity means the valve must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the float assembly inside the tub at the bottom. Lift and release it to ensure it moves freely. Clean any debris preventing movement.

whenToCallPro: If supply pressure is normal and the inlet valve tests good, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E03 water fill error fix

faq.q: Why won't my Thermador dishwasher fill with water?
faq.a: The most common cause is a closed or restricted water supply valve. Check the valve under the sink first, then inspect the inlet hose and filter screen.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E03?
faq.a: Yes. Thermador dishwashers require a minimum of 20 PSI water pressure. Low pressure can trigger E03.

faq.q: How do I reset my Thermador dishwasher after fixing E03?
faq.a: After fixing the issue, disconnect power for 5 minutes, then reconnect and run a normal cycle. The error should clear automatically.

title: Water Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The flow sensor detects insufficient or no water flow through the spray system.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the flow sensor in the Thermador dishwasher is detecting inadequate water circulation through the spray arms. While the tub may have filled with water, the circulation pump is not moving water effectively. This can result from a blocked spray arm, a faulty circulation pump, or a defective flow sensor.

causes.cause: Spray arms are clogged with food debris or mineral deposits
Circulation pump impeller is damaged or jammed
Flow sensor is faulty
Sump filter is severely clogged

symptoms: E04 error on the display
Dishes remain dirty after a full cycle
Weak or no spray from the wash arms
Unusual humming from the circulation pump
Cycle completes but dishes are not wet

fixSteps.title: Clean the spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the upper and lower spray arms. Clear all nozzle holes with a toothpick or thin wire. Rinse under running water to flush debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter assembly from the bottom of the tub. Clean all three filter sections under running water using a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: With filters removed, inspect the pump impeller area for debris or broken glass. Remove any obstructions carefully.

fixSteps.title: Test the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow sensor on the circulation pump housing. Check wiring connections and test with a multimeter for proper resistance values per the service manual.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue and the circulation pump or flow sensor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E04 flow sensor spray arm fix

faq.q: How often should I clean my Thermador dishwasher filters?
faq.a: Clean the filter assembly at least once a month, or weekly if you wash heavily soiled dishes frequently.

faq.q: Can mineral buildup cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. Hard water deposits can clog spray arm nozzles and restrict flow. Use a dishwasher cleaner monthly in hard water areas.

faq.q: Is E04 a serious problem?
faq.a: It often has a simple fix like cleaning spray arms and filters. Only if the pump or sensor is faulty does it require parts replacement.

title: Overflow Protection Activated
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected excess water in the base pan, triggering flood protection.
fullDescription: Error E05 indicates the Thermador dishwasher's AquaStop flood protection system has detected water in the base pan beneath the tub. A float switch in the base activates and triggers the drain pump to remove water while displaying this error. This protects against flooding from leaks in the tub seal, hose connections, or internal components.

causes.cause: Leaking door seal or gasket
Loose or damaged internal hose connection
Cracked sump housing or tub
AquaStop float switch stuck or faulty
Spray arm seal leaking

symptoms: E05 error on the display panel
Drain pump runs continuously even when dishwasher is off
Water visible beneath the dishwasher
Dishwasher will not start a new cycle
Musty smell from under the dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker and shut off the water supply valve. Pull the dishwasher forward to access the base.

fixSteps.title: Remove base panel and dry the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the kick plate and base panel. Use towels and a wet-dry vacuum to remove all water from the base pan.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for the source of the leak
fixSteps.instruction: With the base pan dry, reconnect power and water. Run a short cycle while watching from below with a flashlight to identify the leak source.

fixSteps.title: Check the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door gasket for cracks, tears, or deformation. Clean the seal and the tub edge where it sits. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the AquaStop float in the base pan. Ensure it moves freely up and down. Clean any debris around it. If stuck, gently free it.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source is from a cracked sump or tub, or if you cannot identify where the water is entering the base pan.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E05 leak flood protection fix

faq.q: Why does my Thermador dishwasher drain pump keep running?
faq.a: When E05 triggers, the drain pump activates to remove water from the base pan. It will keep running until the water is removed and the float switch resets.

faq.q: Can I just dry the base pan to fix E05?
faq.a: Drying the base pan will clear the immediate error, but you must find and fix the leak source or the error will return.

faq.q: What is AquaStop on Thermador dishwashers?
faq.a: AquaStop is BSH's flood protection system that uses a float switch in the base pan and an automatic shutoff valve on the supply hose to prevent water damage.

title: Door Switch Fault
shortDescription: The door latch sensor or switch is not registering that the door is properly closed.
fullDescription: Error E06 on Thermador dishwashers means the control board cannot confirm the door is securely latched. The door switch or latch mechanism may be faulty, misaligned, or the wiring to the switch may be damaged. The dishwasher will not start a cycle without confirmation that the door is properly sealed.

causes.cause: Door latch mechanism is misaligned or worn
Door switch has failed electrically
Wiring to the door switch is damaged
Control board door switch input circuit failure

symptoms: E06 error displayed on the panel
Dishwasher does not start when door is closed
Door feels loose when latched
Clicking sound when trying to start but no cycle begins
Intermittent starting issues

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and examine the latch mechanism on the top of the door. Look for visible damage, wear, or misalignment. Check that the strike plate on the tub frame is not bent.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch area
fixSteps.instruction: Clean any detergent buildup or debris from the latch mechanism and strike plate. This can prevent proper engagement.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the door switch behind the inner door panel. Use a multimeter to test for continuity when the switch is pressed. No continuity when activated means the switch is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch or latch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch or latch is faulty, remove the inner door panel, disconnect the old switch, and install the replacement. Reassemble and test.

whenToCallPro: If the latch and switch test good but E06 persists, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E06 door switch latch fix

faq.q: Why won't my Thermador dishwasher start even though the door is closed?
faq.a: The door switch may not be registering the closure. Even if the door feels closed, a worn latch or faulty switch can prevent the signal from reaching the control board.

faq.q: Can I force start the dishwasher with E06?
faq.a: No. The door switch is a safety interlock and the dishwasher will not operate without proper door confirmation.

faq.q: Is E06 covered under Thermador warranty?
faq.a: If your dishwasher is within the standard warranty period, door latch and switch defects are typically covered. Contact Thermador or your dealer.

title: Heating Circuit Relay Failure
shortDescription: The control board's heating circuit relay has failed, preventing the heater from activating.
fullDescription: Error E09 specifically points to a failure in the heating relay circuit on the main control board. Unlike E01 which can indicate the heater or sensor, E09 confirms the control board itself is unable to switch power to the heating element. This is a board-level fault requiring control board repair or replacement.

causes.cause: Heating relay on the control board has failed
Control board solder joint on the relay has cracked
Power surge damaged the relay circuit
Shorted heating element caused relay to burn out

symptoms: E09 error on the display
Water never heats during any cycle
Dishwasher may otherwise function normally
E01 may appear intermittently before E09
Drying performance is very poor

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 10 minutes. Reconnect and run a cycle. If E09 returns immediately, the board relay has failed.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element first
fixSteps.instruction: Before replacing the board, test the heating element for shorts. A shorted element can damage a new board. Measure resistance and ensure no continuity to ground.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door panel to access the control board. Look for visible burn marks, discolored components, or cracked solder joints around the relay area.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct replacement control board. Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting. Install the new board and reconnect all harnesses.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is recommended for professional service, especially if under warranty. Incorrect installation can cause further damage.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E09 control board relay replacement

faq.q: Can the E09 relay be repaired instead of replacing the whole board?
faq.a: Some appliance repair shops can resolder or replace individual relays on the board for less cost. Ask about board-level repair services.

faq.q: How much does a Thermador dishwasher control board cost?
faq.a: A replacement control board typically costs $200-$400 for the part. Professional installation adds $150-$300.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E09?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the relay circuit. Use a surge protector on the dishwasher circuit to prevent future damage.

title: Flow Meter Sensor Error
shortDescription: The flow meter is not detecting proper water intake during the fill cycle.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates the flow meter sensor in the Thermador dishwasher is not registering expected water flow during filling. The flow meter counts water pulses to determine how much water has entered the tub. If the pulse count does not match the expected range, this error triggers.

causes.cause: Flow meter sensor is faulty or blocked
Water supply pressure is too low
Inlet valve is partially clogged
Wiring to the flow meter is damaged

symptoms: E14 error on the display
Dishwasher underfills or overfills
Inconsistent water levels between cycles
Cycle may abort during fill phase
Long fill times before cycle starts

fixSteps.title: Check water supply pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the supply valve is fully open and water pressure is adequate. Run the kitchen faucet to check flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the supply hose and clean the mesh filter at the inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Test the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow meter on the inlet assembly. Disconnect and test with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, remove the old flow meter from the inlet assembly and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the flow meter and inlet valve both test good, the control board may need professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E14 flow meter sensor fix

faq.q: What does the flow meter do in a Thermador dishwasher?
faq.a: The flow meter counts pulses of water entering the tub to precisely measure fill volume. This ensures the correct amount of water for each cycle.

faq.q: Can hard water cause E14?
faq.a: Yes. Mineral deposits from hard water can clog the flow meter or inlet valve filter, restricting flow and triggering E14.

faq.q: Is E14 an urgent repair?
faq.a: It should be addressed promptly as incorrect water levels can lead to poor cleaning or potential overflow conditions.

title: Water in Base Pan (AquaStop)
shortDescription: The AquaStop system has detected water in the base pan, indicating a leak somewhere in the system.
fullDescription: Error E15 is the primary AquaStop flood protection code for Thermador dishwashers. It indicates the float switch in the base pan has risen due to water accumulation. The dishwasher will activate the drain pump and prevent operation until the water is removed and the leak is fixed. This is one of the most common error codes on BSH group dishwashers.

causes.cause: Leaking tub gasket or spray arm seal
Loose or cracked hose connection inside the machine
Sump seal is deteriorated
Condensation buildup from repeated short cycles
AquaStop float switch is stuck

symptoms: E15 flashing on the display
Drain pump runs continuously
Dishwasher will not start any cycle
Water visible under or around the dishwasher
Musty or mildew smell from the base area

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker and close the water supply valve before any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Tilt the dishwasher to drain base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees to drain water from the base pan into towels.

fixSteps.title: Remove base panel and inspect
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the kick plate and base panel. Dry any remaining water. Look for signs of the leak source such as water trails or stains.

fixSteps.title: Check all hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect every hose connection inside the machine. Tighten any loose clamps and replace cracked hoses.

fixSteps.title: Test run and monitor
fixSteps.instruction: With the base panel removed, reconnect power and water. Run a short cycle while monitoring the base area with a flashlight.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the leak source, or if the sump housing is cracked.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E15 AquaStop water in base fix

faq.q: How do I reset E15 on my Thermador dishwasher?
faq.a: Tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees to drain the base pan. This resets the float switch. However, you must fix the underlying leak or E15 will return.

faq.q: Is E15 the same as E05?
faq.a: Both relate to water in the base pan. E15 is the standard AquaStop code while E05 may appear depending on the model. The diagnosis is similar.

faq.q: Can condensation cause E15?
faq.a: In rare cases, excessive condensation from short or cool cycles can accumulate in the base pan. Running a full hot cycle periodically can help prevent this.

title: Overfill Protection Activated
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected excess water in the tub beyond the maximum fill level.
fullDescription: Error E17 indicates the water level in the Thermador dishwasher tub has exceeded the maximum threshold. The overfill protection system triggers the drain pump to remove excess water. This can be caused by a stuck inlet valve, a faulty water level sensor, or a problem with the drain system.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open and not shutting off
Pressure switch or water level sensor is faulty
Drain hose is partially blocked causing residual water buildup
Flow meter is not accurately counting water intake

symptoms: E17 error on the display
Excess water visible in the tub when door is opened
Drain pump runs to remove water
Water on the floor around the dishwasher
Dishwasher aborts the cycle during fill

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off the water supply valve under the sink to stop water from entering the dishwasher. Then disconnect power.

fixSteps.title: Drain excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Let the drain pump run by reconnecting power briefly, or manually bail out excess water from the tub.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the supply off and power disconnected, check the inlet valve. If water still drips in after the valve is shut, the solenoid is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain system
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for clogs or kinks. Ensure the drain hose connects properly with a high loop.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is stuck open, it should be replaced promptly to prevent flooding.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E17 overfill inlet valve fix

faq.q: Is E17 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be. If the inlet valve is stuck open, water will continue to enter the dishwasher. Shut off the water supply valve immediately.

faq.q: Can a blocked drain cause E17?
faq.a: Yes. If the dishwasher cannot drain properly between cycles, residual water can accumulate and trigger the overfill sensor.

faq.q: How do I prevent E17 from happening?
faq.a: Ensure the drain hose has a proper high loop, clean the drain filter regularly, and have the inlet valve inspected if it is more than 5 years old.

title: Clogged Filter or Drain Pump Issue
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not draining properly due to a clogged filter or faulty drain pump.
fullDescription: Error E22 indicates the Thermador dishwasher is unable to drain water from the tub within the expected time. The most common cause is a clogged filter assembly, but a faulty drain pump, blocked drain hose, or garbage disposal blockage can also be responsible.

causes.cause: Filter assembly is clogged with food debris
Drain pump impeller is jammed or faulty
Drain hose is kinked or clogged
Garbage disposal knockout plug was not removed
Check valve in drain line is stuck closed

symptoms: E22 error on the display
Standing water in the bottom of the tub after a cycle
Foul odor from the dishwasher
Dishwasher stops before cycle completion
Gurgling sounds during drain attempts

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack, twist and lift out the cylindrical microfilter and the flat mesh filter. Clean thoroughly under running water with a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose from the dishwasher to the sink drain or disposal. Look for kinks or clogs.

fixSteps.title: Verify garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, ensure the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet was removed during installation.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter and sump cover to access the drain pump impeller. Remove any glass, food, or debris jamming the impeller.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter and drain hose does not resolve the issue and the drain pump needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E22 drain clogged filter fix

faq.q: How do I clean the filter on my Thermador dishwasher?
faq.a: Remove the bottom rack, twist the cylindrical filter counterclockwise and lift out. Remove the flat mesh filter beneath it. Clean both under running water.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with E22?
faq.a: The dishwasher will not complete cycles properly with E22. Standing water can cause odors and mold. Fix the drainage issue before using.

faq.q: What if I just installed a new garbage disposal?
faq.a: When connecting a dishwasher to a new disposal, the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet must be removed. This is the most common cause of E22 after a disposal replacement.

title: Drain Hose Blockage or Kink
shortDescription: The drain system cannot evacuate water due to a blockage or restriction in the drain hose.
fullDescription: Error E24 on Thermador dishwashers is specifically related to the drain hose path. The drain hose may be kinked, improperly installed without a high loop, connected to a clogged garbage disposal, or the air gap may be blocked.

causes.cause: Drain hose is kinked behind or under the dishwasher
Drain hose connection to disposal or drain pipe is blocked
No high loop or air gap in the drain hose installation
Air gap device is clogged (if installed)
Drain pump is weak but still partially functional

symptoms: E24 error on the display
Water backs up into the tub
Slow draining with standing water
Water leaks from under the sink
Cycle completes but tub still has water

fixSteps.title: Inspect drain hose routing
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher forward enough to inspect the drain hose. Straighten any kinks or sharp bends.

fixSteps.title: Ensure proper high loop
fixSteps.instruction: The drain hose must loop up to the underside of the countertop before going down to the drain connection. Secure with a hose clip.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain connection point
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the disposal or drain pipe. Clear any buildup from both the hose end and the connection point.

fixSteps.title: Clean the air gap (if present)
fixSteps.instruction: If your installation has an air gap device on the countertop, remove the cap and clean out any debris inside.

whenToCallPro: If the drain path is clear but E24 persists, the drain pump may be weakening and need professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E24 drain hose blockage fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E22 and E24?
faq.a: E22 is a general drain fault that often points to a clogged filter or faulty pump. E24 specifically indicates a drain hose routing or connection issue.

faq.q: What is a high loop and why does my dishwasher need one?
faq.a: A high loop means the drain hose rises to the underside of the countertop before descending to the drain. This prevents dirty water from siphoning back into the dishwasher.

faq.q: Can I extend the Thermador dishwasher drain hose?
faq.a: The drain hose should not exceed 10 feet total length. If extension is needed, use the same diameter hose and secure connections with proper clamps.

title: Drain Pump Impeller Blocked
shortDescription: The drain pump impeller is obstructed by debris, preventing proper water evacuation.
fullDescription: Error E25 on Thermador dishwashers indicates the drain pump impeller is blocked or unable to rotate freely. Common culprits include broken glass, food particles, toothpicks, or small utensils that have passed through the filter.

causes.cause: Broken glass or hard debris lodged in the pump impeller
Food particles accumulated around the impeller
Pump cover not properly seated after cleaning
Drain pump motor has seized

symptoms: E25 error on the display
No drain pump motor sound during drain cycle
Standing water after every cycle
Buzzing or humming from the pump area
Intermittent draining that gets worse over time

fixSteps.title: Access the drain pump area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack and filter assembly. Remove the sump cover to expose the drain pump impeller area.

fixSteps.title: Remove debris from the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully inspect and remove any glass, food, or foreign objects from around the impeller. Use gloves if broken glass is present.

fixSteps.title: Verify the impeller spins freely
fixSteps.instruction: Gently rotate the impeller by hand. It should spin freely without resistance. If stiff, the pump may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Reassemble and test
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sump cover, filter assembly, and bottom rack. Run a drain cycle to verify E25 has cleared.

whenToCallPro: If the impeller does not spin after clearing debris, the pump motor has likely seized and needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E25 drain pump impeller blocked fix

faq.q: How does debris get to the drain pump?
faq.a: Small items like broken glass, seeds, or food particles can pass through gaps in the filter system and reach the pump impeller.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with E25?
faq.a: No. The dishwasher cannot drain with a blocked impeller. Running it will result in standing water and potential overflow.

faq.q: How do I prevent E25?
faq.a: Scrape dishes thoroughly before loading, clean the filter regularly, and avoid placing items that could break in positions where they might chip.

title: Drying Fan Motor Failure
shortDescription: The drying fan motor is not functioning, preventing proper drying of dishes.
fullDescription: Error E10 on Thermador dishwashers indicates the drying fan motor or its circuit has malfunctioned. Thermador uses a condensation drying system augmented by a fan in many models. When the fan does not operate, dishes remain wet after the drying phase.

causes.cause: Drying fan motor has failed
Fan motor wiring has a loose connection
Control board fan relay has failed
Fan blade is jammed by debris

symptoms: E10 error on the display
Dishes are very wet after the drying phase
No fan sound during the drying cycle
Excessive condensation on the inner door
Cycle completes but drying is ineffective

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan during drying
fixSteps.instruction: Start a cycle and wait for the drying phase. Listen for the fan motor. If silent, the motor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Access the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: The drying fan is typically located in the door or side panel area. Remove the inner door panel or side panel to access it.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor wiring and test with a multimeter for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor tests faulty, remove the mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new motor.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor and wiring test good, the control board fan relay may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E10 drying fan motor replacement

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with E10?
faq.a: Yes. The washing function is not affected by E10. However, dishes will not dry properly and you will need to towel-dry them.

faq.q: Why are my Thermador dishes always wet?
faq.a: If E10 is displayed, the drying fan is not working. If no error is shown, try using the Extra Dry option and a rinse aid.

faq.q: Does E10 affect energy efficiency?
faq.a: Not directly, but running the dishwasher without effective drying means you may need to re-run cycles or dry dishes manually.

title: Diverter Valve Malfunction
shortDescription: The wash zone diverter valve is not switching correctly between upper and lower spray arms.
fullDescription: Error E19 on Thermador dishwashers indicates the diverter valve that directs water between the upper and lower spray arms is not functioning correctly. When it fails, one set of spray arms may not receive water, leading to poor cleaning on those racks.

causes.cause: Diverter valve motor has failed
Diverter valve is jammed by debris
Wiring to the diverter valve is damaged
Control board signal to diverter is faulty

symptoms: E19 error on the display
Top rack dishes not cleaned properly
Bottom rack dishes not cleaned properly
Only one spray arm seems to be working
Clicking or grinding sound from the diverter area

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes. Sometimes the diverter valve motor gets stuck temporarily.

fixSteps.title: Access the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: The diverter is in the sump area. Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filter assembly to access it.

fixSteps.title: Clean and inspect the diverter
fixSteps.instruction: Check for debris around the valve. Clean any buildup. Try to manually move the diverter.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the motor electrically. If faulty, disconnect and replace the diverter valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the diverter motor tests good but the valve is still not switching, the control board may need attention.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E19 diverter valve fix

faq.q: What does the diverter valve do?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between the upper and lower spray arms during the wash cycle for thorough cleaning of all rack levels.

faq.q: Can I bypass the diverter valve?
faq.a: No. Without the diverter, water would only flow to one spray zone, resulting in poor cleaning.

faq.q: Is E19 common on Thermador dishwashers?
faq.a: It is less common than drain or heating errors but does occur, especially in models over 3-4 years old.
